title: Indulgent Slovakian Pierogi
description: Citrusy and crumbly Slovakian pierogi featuring a tangy chimichurri sauce and caramelized onions, perfect as a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and eggs to make the pierogi dough.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 2 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin sheet.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of caramelized onions and a sprinkle of feta cheese in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with milk and fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the pierogi and use a fork to crimp the edges.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi for 5 minutes or until they float to the surface.
Drain the pierogi and serve with a dollop of chimichurri sauce and a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese.
To make the chimichurri sauce, combine parsley, oregano, garlic, and butter in a blender and blend until smooth.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
